question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DomainUtils.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getHashBytes(HashObject hashObject)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a record stream file containing a HashObject with a maximally large hash ByteString, causing getHashBytes() to call toBytes() which allocates a full copy of the hash data, and under high file volume exhaust heap memory in the importer?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DomainUtils.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: createSha384Digest()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a code path that calls createSha384Digest() in a tight loop (e.g., by submitting many transactions requiring hash verification), causing repeated MessageDigest.getInstance() calls which involve security provider lookup and synchronization, creating a CPU bottleneck and degrading importer throughput?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DomainUtils.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: fromEvmAddress(byte[] evmAddress)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract transaction with an EVM address that passes isLongZeroAddress() but whose last 8 bytes decode to a num value that causes EntityId.of() to throw InvalidEntityException, causing fromEvmAddress() to log a debug message per transaction and under high volume exhaust log buffer capacity?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DomainUtils.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: fromTrimmedEvmAddress(byte[] evmAddress)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call with a trimmed EVM address of length 1-19 bytes, causing fromTrimmedEvmAddress() to allocate a new padding byte array and call Bytes.concat() to create a new 20-byte array, and under high volume cause excessive short-lived object allocation that degrades GC performance?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DomainUtils.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: toEvmAddress(ContractID contractId)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract transaction with a ContractID whose EVM_ADDRESS case contains a maximally large ByteString, causing toEvmAddress() to call toBytes() which allocates a full copy of the EVM address data, and under high volume exhaust heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DomainUtils.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: toEvmAddress(ContractID contractId)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a ContractID set to the default instance, causing toEvmAddress() to call ContractID.getDefaultInstance().equals(contractId) which performs a full protobuf equality check, and if this is called in a tight loop throw InvalidEntityException repeatedly, causing exception handling overhead to degrade importer performance?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DomainUtils.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isLongZeroAddress(byte[] evmAddress)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a high volume of contract transactions with EVM addresses that are exactly 20 bytes but not long-zero addresses, causing isLongZeroAddress() to call Arrays.equals() for every transaction, and under extreme volume the cumulative array comparison cost contributes to CPU saturation?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DomainUtils.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: trim(byte[] data)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HookStorageChange with a valueRead or valueWritten byte array that is maximally large and starts with a single non-zero byte followed by all zeros, causing trim() to return the full array via ArrayUtils.subarray() which allocates a new copy, doubling memory usage per storage change under high volume?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DomainUtils.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: trim(byte[] data)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract state change with a value byte array that is maximally large and has a single leading zero byte, causing trim() to call ArrayUtils.subarray(data, 1, data.length) which allocates a new array of nearly the same size, and under high volume cause heap exhaustion through repeated near-full-size array copies?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DomainUtils.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getPublicKey(byte[] protobufKey)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CryptoCreate transaction with a protobufKey that is a valid KEYLIST containing a single KEYLIST containing a single primitive key (depth=3), causing getPublicKey(Key, depth) to return null at depth>2 but only after Key.parseFrom() has already allocated the full nested key structure in memory, wasting heap per transaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DomainUtils.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getPublicKey(byte[] protobufKey)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a protobufKey byte array that is syntactically invalid protobuf but very large, causing Key.parseFrom() to throw an exception after consuming significant CPU time parsing the malformed input, and the catch block at line 104 logs an error, causing log I/O overhead under high volume?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DomainUtils.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: sanitize(String input)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a FileCreate or FileUpdate transaction with a file memo containing a multi-megabyte string of null characters, causing sanitize() to allocate a new String of equal size with replacement characters, and if the importer processes multiple such transactions concurrently, exhaust heap memory and crash the JVM?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DomainUtils.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: toBytes(ByteString byteString)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction containing a composite ByteString (RopeByteString) formed by concatenating many small ByteStrings, causing UnsafeByteOutput.supports() to return false (wrong class type) and toBytes() to fall through to byteString.toByteArray() which must linearize the rope structure, consuming O(n) CPU and allocating a new array?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DomainUtils.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: normalize(ContractSlotKey slotKey)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a smart contract with a storage slot key that is a large ByteString starting with zero bytes, causing normalize() to call toBytes() (allocation), trim() (O(n) scan + subarray allocation), and fromBytes() (wrap), creating three heap allocations per slot normalization and under high SSTORE volume causing GC pressure that halts the importer?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DomainUtils.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: leftPadBytes(byte[] bytes, int length)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HookStorage key update where the key byte array is empty (length 0) but the KEY_BYTE_LENGTH=32 padding is applied, and by submitting millions of such transactions cause leftPadBytes() to allocate 32-byte arrays at a rate that overwhelms the GC's ability to reclaim them, causing a GC pause storm?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DomainUtils.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: convertToNanosMax(long second, long nanos)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a timestamp where second=Long.MAX_VALUE and nanos=Long.MAX_VALUE, causing convertToNanosMax() to catch the ArithmeticException from convertToNanos() and silently return Long.MAX_VALUE, but the exception creation and stack trace generation still consume CPU, and under high volume this overhead degrades importer throughput?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DomainUtils.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: convertToNanosMax(Instant instant)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger convertToNanosMax(Instant) with a null Instant, causing it to substitute Instant.EPOCH and proceed, but if this is called in a tight loop with null inputs the repeated null check and Instant.EPOCH substitution creates unnecessary object references that increase GC pressure?
